subject: Environment
text: Kaziranga National Park, a UNESCO World Heritage Site, is primarily known for which animal?
explanation: Kaziranga, in Golaghat and Nagaon districts of Assam, is home to two-thirds of the world's Indian One-Horned Rhinoceros population. It also has the highest density of tigers among protected areas.

subject: Geography
text: The Brahmaputra river is known as the "Sorrow of Assam" because:
options: It causes devastating annual floods in Assam
explanation: The Brahmaputra is called the "Sorrow of Assam" because of the severe and recurring floods it causes each year, displacing millions and causing immense loss of life and property. The river is also prone to frequent channel migration (avulsion).

subject: Art & Culture
text: How many times is Bihu celebrated in Assam in a year?
explanation: Bihu is celebrated three times a year: Rongali (Bohag) Bihu in April (spring), Kongali (Kati) Bihu in October (autumn), and Bhogali (Magh) Bihu in January (winter harvest). Rongali Bihu is the most celebrated.

subject: Art & Culture
text: In which year did Sattriya receive the status of Classical Dance from the Sangeet Natak Akademi?
explanation: Sattriya was granted classical status in November 2000. Originating in the 15th-century Vaishnavite monasteries (Sattras) of Assam, it was codified by the saint-scholar Srimanta Sankardeva.

subject: Economy
text: The first oil well in Asia was drilled at which place in Assam?
explanation: Digboi in Tinsukia district is where Asia's first oil well was drilled in 1867. The Digboi Refinery (1901) is also the oldest operating oil refinery in Asia and is still in production today.

subject: Geography
text: Which is the largest district in Assam by area?
explanation: Karbi Anglong is the largest district in Assam, covering approximately 10,434 sq km. It is predominantly a tribal district and is home to the Karbi and Dimasa people.

subject: History
text: The Ahom Kingdom ruled Assam for approximately how many years before British annexation?
explanation: The Ahom Kingdom ruled from 1228 AD (under Sukaphaa) until 1826 AD when the Treaty of Yandabo brought Assam under British control — a continuous reign of approximately 600 years. They repelled Mughal invasions 17 times.

subject: History
text: The Battle of Saraighat (1671) was fought between the Ahom Kingdom and:
explanation: The Battle of Saraighat was fought on the Brahmaputra river in 1671. Ahom commander Lachit Borphukan decisively defeated the Mughal fleet under Ram Singh I, halting Mughal expansion into Assam. Lachit Divas is celebrated on November 24.

subject: Polity
text: Which Schedule of the Indian Constitution provides for the administration of tribal areas in Assam?
explanation: The Sixth Schedule provides for autonomous district councils in tribal areas of Assam, Meghalaya, Tripura, and Mizoram. These councils have legislative, judicial, and executive powers over specified matters.

subject: Polity
text: What is the legislative capital (seat of the state legislature) of Assam?
explanation: Dispur, located within the Guwahati metropolitan area, is the capital of Assam where the Assam Legislative Assembly is situated. Guwahati is the largest city and commercial hub, but the administrative capital is Dispur.
